Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: sync hardening with exponential backoff + dead letter queue (Phase 3)

- New syncEngine.ts: drainSyncQueue() with batch processing (10 items/cycle),
  exponential backoff (15s->30s->60s->120s->300s), max 10 retries per item
  before moving to failedQueue (dead letter)
- Conflict resolution: table status = server wins (updated_at comparison),
  open orders = client wins (waiter device is source of truth),
  paid/cancelled orders = server wins (fiscal receipt authoritative)
- ConnectivityMonitor: separate drain timer with backoff (was inline in 15s poll),
  immediate drain on online event, concurrent drain guard
- Store: added failedSyncs, lastSyncedAt state + setters
- UI: tables page shows failed sync badge (red) + last sync time,
  settings page shows dead queue count + timestamp

commit.message:
Restart Windows MIDI Service at startup for LoopMIDI port detection (v3.2.22)

Windows MIDI Services (KB5074105, rolled out Feb-Mar 2026) doesn't detect
virtual MIDI ports created by LoopMIDI before the service starts. Restarting
midisrv at startup forces re-enumeration so ports are visible to python-rtmidi.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: skip unreadable files in checksum calculation and zip creation

Files owned by root (e.g. __pycache__ created by live-dev containers)
cause checksum mismatches because the client includes them but cannot
read their contents. Skip unreadable files/directories in all four
code paths: calculateGitTreeHashRecursive, calculateMergedGitTreeHash,
zipDirectory, and createStreamingZip.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add HTML+CSS output and GitHub Pages deployment

Add HTML renderer with responsive CSS, dark mode, and inline SVG chord
diagrams. Add site builder to convert a directory of .cho files into a
static HTML site with index page. Add build-site CLI subcommand and
--format html support. Include GitHub Actions workflow for Pages deploy.
Bump version to 0.2.0.

commit.message:
feat: add SQLite offline-first database layer (Phase 1)

Replace Dexie/IndexedDB with @capacitor-community/sqlite on native
platforms (iOS/Android) while keeping Dexie as web fallback.

- dbTypes.ts: shared interfaces + UnifiedDb type definition
- dexieDb.ts: pure Dexie class (web fallback + migration source)
- sqliteDb.ts: SQLite adapter + Dexie adapter + one-time migration
- waiterDb.ts: thin re-export layer (zero breaking changes)
- DbInitializer: calls initDb() at app startup
- Runtime detection: Capacitor.isNativePlatform() picks backend
- One-time Dexie->SQLite data migration on first native boot
- All existing imports and function signatures unchanged

commit.message:
fix: ensure Zeroizing wrapper is dropped after X3DHKeyPair::from_bytes

Bind secret_bytes() to a local variable so the Zeroizing wrapper
outlives the dereference, ensuring the stack copy is zeroized on drop.

commit.message:
Fix deploy-viewer Pages URL detection for new forks

The gh api call returns a 404 JSON body on stdout when Pages has no
prior deployment, which --jq does not filter on error responses. Use
jq // empty and explicit fallback to handle this cleanly.

commit.message:
fix: dereference Zeroizing wrapper in X3DHKeyPair::from_bytes calls

secret_bytes() returns Zeroizing<[u8; 32]> but from_bytes() expects
[u8; 32]. The dereference operator extracts the inner array.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add asset diff viewer to promotion manager

Adds cross-stage compare buttons (Dev↔Staging, Staging↔Production) and
per-history-item "View Diff" buttons. Diffs are fetched from the gitops
backend and rendered in a color-coded webview panel with word-diff toggle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Release: fix infinite render loop (React Error #185) (#900)

* Add live config switching for mid-survey sensor reconfiguration (closes #753) (#831)

When an operator switches sensor configuration mid-survey (e.g., inline-4
to xwing-5), the system now: completes the current Acquisition, creates a
new one with the new config, dispatches the correct kernel for subsequent
points, and the compositor accumulates estimates from both configurations.

Changes:
- LaneA: add set_config() to update active config_name/geometry_name
- LaneA._resolve_kernel: use _config_name for direct kernel lookup with
  fallback to first available pathfinder kernel
- LaneA._process: emit config_name in PropertyEstimate
- SignalInterpreter: add set_config() delegating to LaneA
- StreamingService._handle_corrected_message: on config change, update
  interpreter config and acquisition_id after session transition
- 24 tests covering detection, session transitions, compositor
  accumulation, kernel dispatch, multiple switches, and end-to-end flow

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add external import metadata schema for multi-instrument data fusion (#828)

Implement ExternalImportConfig, import service, and API endpoints for
importing external geophysical data (GPR, metal detector, EMI, resistivity)
into the Bayesian fusion system. Includes column mapping, file preview,
import templates, and 19 passing tests. Closes #735

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: per-kernel training data generation (closes #754) (#834)

Generate training data where the same buried scene is observed with
different sensor configs (inline-4, xwing-5, cross-8). Matched chips
share ground truth but produce different observed signals, enabling
kernel-transfer learning experiments.

- KernelTrainingConfig: per-kernel simulation parameters
- TrainingChip: observation + ground truth + metadata
- generate_per_kernel_chips(): one scene x M configs -> M matched chips
- PerKernelDataset: N scenes x M configs with JSONL manifest
- generate_random_scene(): standalone dipole scene generator
- 16 tests covering reproducibility, matched pairs, dimensions, noise

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add CRS normalization for external datasets (closes #736) (#835)

CRSNormalizer class detects and transforms coordinates from external CRS
(WGS84, UTM, State Plane, etc.) to the mission's local tangent plane.
Supports EPSG, WKT, and PROJ4 input formats with heuristic auto-detection.
24 tests covering transforms, round-trips, and edge cases.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add layer manager panel with per-acquisition isolation (closes #759) (#838)

Zustand layerStore for per-acquisition visibility/opacity/solo/mute state,
LayerManagerPanel sheet with composite fused layer at top, TanStack Query
hooks for acquisitions and fusion status APIs, toolbar Layers button,
and 14 unit tests for layer state logic.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add factorized synthetic data generator architecture (closes #751) (#839)

Factor ScenarioGenerator into three independent axes to avoid
Cartesian explosion when adding new sensor configurations:

- SceneGenerator: targets + clutter + soil + terrain (seeded)
- ObservationGenerator: applies MeasurementKernel via registry
- NoiseInjector: white + 1/f per-instrument noise model
- FactorizedGenerator: orchestrator with generate_chip() and
  generate_matched_pair() for config-aware training

27 tests covering reproducibility, matched pairs, and end-to-end.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add GPR time-slice import adapter (closes #741) (#842)

Implements a dedicated GPR import pipeline supporting CSV grid data and
basic SEG-Y time-slice exports. Maps GPR amplitude values to property
estimates via configurable gain scaling, with depth-slice-aware voxel
mapping for multi-layer surveys.

New files:
- app/services/gpr_import.py — GPRImportConfig, CSV/SEG-Y parsers,
  amplitude-to-property conversion, import_gpr_timeslice()
- app/routers/gpr_import.py — POST /api/import/gpr and /gpr/preview
- tests/test_gpr_import.py — 34 tests covering parsing, import logic,
  depth mapping, compositor integration, and API endpoints

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add multi-pass training scenario generator (closes #756) (#843)

Implement MultiPassGenerator for generating training data with repeated
coverage over the same scene using different survey parameters (line
spacing, direction, position offset, noise level). The ML model learns
that repeated coverage reduces uncertainty.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Live fusion state delta broadcast via WebSocket, closes #757 (#847)

Emit a distinct `fusion_state_delta` WebSocket event alongside the
existing `signal_volume_update`. The new event carries per-property
sparse posterior deltas (indices, mean, variance, confidence) plus
per-voxel dominant-source evidence for real-time layer isolation.

Backend:
- streaming.py: _process_batch returns (volume_result, fusion_delta)
  tuple; _interpreter_consumer broadcasts both events independently;
  new _build_evidence_summary extracts dominant acquisition per voxel
- ws.py: subscribe_fusion / unsubscribe_fusion message types for
  client-side property-type filtering

Frontend:
- types/streaming.ts: FusionStateDelta, FusionPropertyDelta,
  FusionEvidenceSummary interfaces + isFusionStateDelta guard
- stores/fusionDeltaStore.ts: Zustand store maintaining per-property
  Float32Arrays and dominant-source strings from incremental deltas
- hooks/useWebSocket.ts: dispatch fusion_state_delta to the new store

Tests: 20 new tests covering delta format, evidence summary, process
batch return shape, WebSocket subscription, and version tracking.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Live fusion state delta broadcast via WebSocket, closes #757 (#848)

Emit a distinct `fusion_state_delta` WebSocket event alongside the
existing `signal_volume_update`. The new event carries per-property
sparse posterior deltas (indices, mean, variance, confidence) plus
per-voxel dominant-source evidence for real-time layer isolation.

Backend:
- streaming.py: _process_batch returns (volume_result, fusion_delta)
  tuple; _interpreter_consumer broadcasts both events independently;
  new _build_evidence_summary extracts dominant acquisition per voxel
- ws.py: subscribe_fusion / unsubscribe_fusion message types for

Frontend:
- types/streaming.ts: FusionStateDelta, FusionPropertyDelta,

- stores/fusionDeltaStore.ts: Zustand store maintaining per-property
  Float32Arrays and dominant-source strings from incremental deltas
- hooks/useWebSocket.ts: dispatch fusion_state_delta to the new store

Tests: 20 new tests covering delta format, evidence summary, process
batch return shape, WebSocket subscription, and version tracking.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add multi-instrument concurrent streaming (closes #755) (#849)

Refactor StreamingService to support simultaneous Pathfinder + HIRT
data streams, each with its own acquisition session and
SignalInterpreter, all sharing a single BayesianCompositor for
cross-instrument fusion.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add evidence contribution heatmap overlay, closes #764 (#852)

Per-voxel dominant-source categorical colormap overlay showing which
acquisition contributes most confidence at each grid cell. Includes
12-color perceptually distinct palette, TanStack Query data hook,
deck.gl BitmapLayer rendering, toolbar/keyboard toggle (E key), and
floating legend. 16 new tests covering colormap assignment, dominance
computation, and appStore integration.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add metal detector signal import adapter (closes #744) (#853)

Implement metal detector CSV import with signal-to-chi sensitivity curve
mapping (linear, logarithmic, quadratic). Variance inversely proportional
to signal strength for confidence-weighted fusion via BayesianCompositor.

New files:
- import_adapters/metal_detector_adapter.py: MetalDetectorImportConfig,
  import_metal_detector(), sensitivity curves, signal-to-variance mapping
- POST /api/import/metal-detector endpoint in external_imports router
- 16 tests covering all 3 curves, variance model, custom columns, zero
  signal handling, compositor integration, and edge cases

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Streaming provenance: real-time lineage at each pipeline stage (#857)

Record provenance events at every streaming pipeline stage —
capture, tilt_correction, kernel_dispatch, property_estimation,
and fusion — providing a complete audit trail linked to each
acquisition_id.

- Add StreamingProvenanceEvent dataclass and record_streaming_event()
  to ProvenanceStore for lightweight append-only streaming events
- Add find_recent() for monitoring recent provenance across all
  acquisitions
- Integrate fire-and-forget provenance recording into StreamingService
  at capture/correction (in _handle_corrected_message) and at
  kernel_dispatch/estimation/fusion (in _process_batch)
- Add GET /api/provenance/{acquisition_id} and
  GET /api/provenance/recent/events API endpoints
- 22 tests covering all stages, chain integrity, query, performance,
  and API endpoints

closes #762

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add per-acquisition property grids on 2D map (closes #761) (#856)

Backend: new endpoint GET /api/fusion/state/contribution/{id}/grid that
projects sparse 3D voxel contributions to a dense 2D grid via top-layer
projection. Returns NaN for uncovered cells with confidence array.

Frontend: useAcquisitionGrid/useAcquisitionGrids hooks fetch per-acquisition
grids with version-keyed caching. AcquisitionGridLayer renders BitmapLayers
with confidence-weighted alpha transparency. Layer store gains activeView
('composite'|'per-acquisition') that auto-switches when solo is toggled.
MapView conditionally renders acquisition grids or composite heatmap.

Also adds useFusionData hook (composite/snapshot queries) and fusion
composite/snapshot types to api.ts.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Per-acquisition property grids on 2D map (#761) (#862)

* Add per-acquisition property grids on 2D map (closes #761)

Backend: new endpoint GET /api/fusion/state/contribution/{id}/grid that
projects sparse 3D voxel contributions to a dense 2D grid via top-layer
projection. Returns NaN for uncovered cells with confidence array.

Frontend: useAcquisitionGrid/useAcquisitionGrids hooks fetch per-acquisition

Also adds useFusionData hook (composite/snapshot queries) and fusion

Adds PipelineStage enum for type-safe pipeline stage references, injectable

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Per-kernel conditioned denoiser with FiLM conditioning (#760) (#868)

* Add GPR time-slice import adapter (closes #741)

GPR time-slice CSV import with time-to-depth conversion, amplitude
normalization, and depth-layer mapping to the voxel mesh. Extends
ExternalImportConfig with GPRImportConfig (time_slice_ns, velocity,
optional explicit depth). POST /api/import/gpr endpoint. 34 tests.

* Add per-kernel conditioned denoiser with FiLM conditioning (closes #760)

Kernel-conditioned UNet architecture: shared encoder backbone with FiLM
(Feature-wise Linear Modulation) conditioning per encoder stage and
lightweight per-kernel decoder heads. Enables a single model to
specialize denoising for different sensor geometries (inline-4, xwing-5,
EMI HCP/VCP) via kernel ID embedding.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add training manifest with acquisition metadata for config-aware data loading (#870)

Implement ManifestEntry dataclass and TrainingManifest class with JSONL
persistence, filtering by config/kernel/instrument, stratified sampling
for balanced training batches, and summary statistics. Integrate with
PerKernelDataset to emit training_manifest.jsonl alongside training chips.
42 tests covering round-trip, filtering, sampling, edge cases, and
generator integration.

closes #763

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Rewrite multi-pass training generator with spec-matching API (#756) (#874)

Replace the old FactorizedGenerator-based multi-pass API
(MultiPassGenerator/MultiPassSample/PassMetadata) with the spec-matching
API: PassConfig, MultiPassConfig, generate_multi_pass_chips(),
MultiPassDataset. The new implementation uses SceneSpec from
per_kernel_generator and boustrophedon survey paths with IDW gridding.

Key changes:
- PassConfig dataclass (direction_deg, line_spacing_m, noise_std, speed_m_s)
- MultiPassConfig dataclass (passes, grid_size, kernel_name)
- generate_multi_pass_chips() returns progressive fusion dicts with
  individual_grid, cumulative_grid, uncertainty_map, ground_truth
- MultiPassDataset.generate() batch-generates NPZ chips + JSONL manifest
- 19 tests covering direction diversity, uncertainty reduction,
  orthogonal vs parallel passes, line spacing, reproducibility,
  chip structure, and dataset generation

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: config-aware tooltip with acquisition details (closes #765) (#875)

Replace generic {x, y, gradient_nt} tooltip with rich AcquisitionTooltip
showing fused posterior (mean +/- std), confidence bar, and per-acquisition
breakdown (instrument, config, estimate, weight). Debounced 100ms fetch
via useVoxelEvidence hook with TanStack Query caching.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add kernel sensitivity volume visualization in 3D scene, closes #767 (#876)

Translucent voxel rendering of measurement kernel spatial sensitivity
patterns using InstancedMesh for GPU-efficient rendering (1000-5000 voxels).
Supports gradiometer (1/r^3 dipole) and EMI (McNeill LIN HCP/VCP) kernels
with cool-to-warm colormap (blue->cyan->green->yellow->red). Toggled via
toolbar "Kernel" button in the 3D view.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add multi-step import wizard with acquisition metadata collection closes #752 (#880)

Four-step Sheet wizard (Source > File > Metadata > Confirm) for importing
external geophysical data (GPR, metal detector, EMI, resistivity, magnetic
gradiometer). Collects operator, date, CRS, sensor height, and notes.
Preview endpoint auto-detects columns and CRS before submission.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add fusion timeline for progressive clarification replay (closes #768) (#882)

Implements a horizontal timeline bar that replays fusion state snapshots
as acquisitions are added. Users can scrub left/right to see how the
composite posterior evolved over time, with play/pause animation support
and per-version confidence display.

New files:
- timelineStore.ts: Zustand store tracking timeline events, version, playback
- useFusionTimeline.ts: Hook fetching acquisitions, driving animation loop
- FusionTimeline.tsx: Timeline bar with acquisition markers and transport controls

Integration:
- api.ts: Add fetchConfidenceDelta for confidence tracking at each version
- OverlaysMenu: Toggle for Fusion Timeline overlay (shortcut F)
- SplitWorkspace: Render FusionTimeline above TimelineScrubber
- AcquisitionGridLayer: Filter visible grids by timeline version when scrubbing
- MapView: Show version indicator badge when viewing historical fusion state

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add multi-channel EM (Dualem, EM38) import adapter (#884)

Implement FDEM data import with multiple coil separations. Each coil
config (separation, HCP/VCP mode, frequency) produces a separate
PropertyEstimate for the Bayesian compositor.

- em_import.py: EMImportConfig, CoilConfig, import_em_data(), presets
  for EM38 (2 ch), Dualem-21S (4 ch), Dualem-421S (6 ch)
- Router: POST /api/import/em + GET /api/import/em/presets
- 30 tests covering presets, multi-channel, sigma conversion, variance,
  error handling, and API endpoints

closes #747

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add LiDAR point cloud to DEM pipeline (closes #718) (#886)

LAS 1.2 binary parser (format 0/1), ASPRS classification filtering,
grid-to-heightmap with mean elevation per cell, nearest-neighbor gap
interpolation. POST /api/terrain/import/lidar endpoint. Optional LAZ
via laspy[lazrs]. 43 tests covering parser, filter, gridding, API.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implements confidence delta visualization showing how re-walking
an area improves survey confidence. Includes:

- divergingColormap.ts: Blue-White-Red scale for delta values
- useConfidenceDelta.ts: Hook fetching /api/fusion/confidence-delta
- ConfidenceDeltaOverlay.tsx: deck.gl BitmapLayer for delta overlay
- ComparisonPanel.tsx: Stats panel (mean improvement, area affected)
- ComparisonView: "Re-Walk" mode with map + stats split
- CommandPalette: "Re-Walk Comparison" command
- OverlaysMenu: "Confidence Delta" toggle

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add GeoTIFF DEM import for pre-survey terrain (closes #715)

Implement single-band Float32 GeoTIFF parsing without GDAL dependency.
Parse CRS from GeoKeys, reproject to local grid via CRSNormalizer,
handle nodata values, and optionally resample to target resolution.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix backend bugs: contribution lookup, evidence compositor, duplicate routes, stale tests (#897)

- Fix fusion router contribution lookup: iterate contributions by
  acquisition_id instead of direct key lookup with canonical type,
  which failed for sigma/rho queries (raw key != canonical key)
- Fix evidence router: delegate to streaming_service._compositor
  instead of using a disconnected module-level singleton
- Remove duplicate POST /api/import/gpr route from external_imports.py
  that shadowed the more complete gpr_import.py implementation
- Fix test_mqtt_acquisition_metadata: destructure _process_batch
  tuple return (volume_result, fusion_delta) instead of dict access

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix infinite render loop crashing production (React Error #185) (#899)

Root cause: LayerManagerPanel's `acqData?.items ?? []` created a new
array reference on every render while the acquisitions query was loading.
This fired the syncLayers useEffect on each render, which always created
a new Map in layerStore (even when content was unchanged), triggering
another re-render — an infinite loop.

Three fixes:
1. Stabilize acquisitions reference with useMemo in LayerManagerPanel
2. Make layerStore.syncLayers bail out early when no changes needed
3. Fix useAcquisitionGrids useMemo dependency on unstable useQueries array

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: code quality and security hardening from scan findings (#2)

Remediate all findings from code scan (22 findings) and quick-win
security scan findings (7 of 19). Test count goes from 15 to 43.

Code scan fixes:
- Deduplicate proxy route handlers into makeHandler() factory
- Import ENGINE_URL from constants (remove duplication)
- Add thread-safe double-checked locking to DB/Redis singletons
- Health endpoint returns 503 when degraded (was 200)
- Wire all schemas to BaseSchema (populate_by_name config)
- Add CORS headers to CatchAllMiddleware error responses
- Add ORM relationships with cascade delete to all FK models
- setup_logging() respects debug flag (ConsoleRenderer vs JSON)
- ENGINE_VERSION derived from importlib.metadata
- Fix test fixture global app mutation with proper teardown
- Add cross-reference comments for shared cache TTL constants

Security quick wins:
- Strip sensitive headers in proxy (Cookie, Authorization, etc.)
- Add security headers (CSP, X-Frame-Options, HSTS, nosniff, etc.)
- Normalize proxy paths to prevent traversal attacks
- Reject wildcard CORS origins in production mode
- Explicit CORS allow_headers (was wildcard)
- Use uv sync --frozen in CI and Dockerfile
- Expand .dockerignore to exclude secrets and build artifacts

New tests (28 added):
- Vitest setup with 14 TypeScript tests (proxy allowlist, engine
  client, shared constants)
- 14 new Python tests (config validation, logging renderer, schema
  base class, CORS on error, version consistency)

fix: add STL compat shims for missing MSVC vectorized find functions

moonshine-utils.lib (v0.0.49) was compiled with a newer MSVC that has
vectorized __std_find_first_not_of_trivial_pos_1 and
__std_find_last_not_of_trivial_pos_1, but these symbols don't exist in
the MSVC STL on the CI runner (VS 2022 17.14).

Provide simple C fallback implementations that perform the same
byte-level find-not-of operation. These are compiled and linked into
moonshine.dll during the CI build.

commit.message:
fix: build moonshine.dll from static libs in CI instead of static linking

Static linking failed due to CRT mismatch — moonshine .lib files are
compiled with /MD (dynamic CRT) but Rust links /MT, and the MSVC STL
vectorized symbols aren't available in the Rust link environment.

New approach: CI uses the MSVC linker to create moonshine.dll from the
static .lib files + a .def file listing exports. This lets MSVC resolve
all STL dependencies correctly with its own toolchain. The resulting
moonshine.dll is bundled alongside onnxruntime.dll in the installer.

Rust code goes back to dynamic loading via libloading on all platforms.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

v1.3.0: Add framework config detection for CVE suppression

The build hook now detects Next.js runtime config (PPR, serverActions)
by reading next.config.{ts,js,mjs} via regex and sends it alongside
the dependency list. The server uses this to suppress vulnerabilities
that aren't exploitable for the project's configuration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Multi-version mod support, UUID auto-registration, Cloudflare Worker

  via GitHub Contents API (deploy once, GITHUB_TOKEN stored as Worker secret)
- nl-users.json: empty registry file in repo root (auto-updated by Worker)
- Both mod JARs uploaded to GitHub Releases v1.0.0

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Obsidian vault sync backend + Settings UI

Add ObsidianSyncBackend implementing SyncableBackend protocol for
continuous incremental sync of Obsidian vault notes. Edit-as-activity
model: edits create new journal segments at edit time, building a
history of engagement with each note.

Backend (obsidian.py):
- Two-level change detection: mtime fast path, SHA-256 content hash
- New notes: full content import via existing parsing, segment at mtime
- Edited notes: new segment at current mtime, edit_count tracked
- Deleted notes: status "removed", journal segments preserved
- Vault auto-detection: ~/Documents/Obsidian/, ~/Obsidian/, or --path
- Sync state at {journal}/imports/obsidian.json
- Registered as "obsidian" in SYNCABLE_REGISTRY

UI (Settings > Sync):
- Obsidian Vault card alongside Plaud and Granola
- Toggle for hourly sync schedule
- GET/PUT /api/sync endpoints handle obsidian

CLI: sol import --sync obsidian [--save] [--force] [--path /custom]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix: crane beam M1 — correct αпб table and kпб=1.2 per ЦНИИ methodology

- CRANE_BEAM_ALPHA updated to match methodology ranges:
  Q=20–50t: αпб=0.24–0.35; Q=80–200t: αпб=0.37–0.47 (linear interpolation).
  Example confirmed: Q=100t → αпб=0.39.
- kпб changed 1.4 → 1.2 in 6 places (main_desktop.py ×3, main.py ×3).
- TestEtalonBlok3CraneBeamM1: etalon updated 41.54t → 45.91t.
- Docs: VERIFICATION_PLAN Block 3 closed, KNOWLEDGE_BASE formula corrected,
  CONTEXT_RECOVERY open question #1 resolved.
- 132 passed, 0 failed.
